question_text: If the accused pleads guilty, the punishment shall be ______.

options.D: At the discretion of the Court

correct_answer_text: D) At the discretion of the Court

question_text: Under which section of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 can a Magistrate personally arrest a person?
A charge framed by the Court shall be written in the ________ language.
How many classes of Judges shall there be in each Sessions Division?
When a police officer arrests a person by warrant, the person must be produced before the Court ________.
If the Government confers special powers, what is the maximum term of imprisonment a Metropolitan Magistrate can impose?
Which of the following is NOT a power of an appellate court in a criminal appeal?
Which of the following shall NOT be mentioned in the written directions of a warrant?

options.A: Quantum of punishment for the offence
options.B: Date of appearance before the Court

correct_answer_text: A) Quantum of punishment for the offence

question_text: Death may be caused in arresting a person if he is accused of an offence punishable with ________ imprisonment.
Who can conduct a 'Local Inquiry' under the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898?
After framing of charge against the accused, what is NOT done?

options.B: Asking whether the accused pleads guilty
options.C: Asking the accused about defence evidence

correct_answer_text: C) Asking the accused about defence evidence

question_text: Which section of the Penal Code, 1860 cannot be tried summarily?
Under section 145 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, if a party to the proceedings dies —

options.C: Representatives of the deceased may become parties

correct_answer_text: C) Representatives of the deceased may become parties

question_text: What is the maximum fine a Second Class Magistrate can impose (in Taka)?
If a case is settled through compromise, it shall be treated as the accused's ________.
Under which section of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 are non-cognizable cases mentioned?
Under section 144 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, who may issue an order?
Which of the following presented to a Magistrate is NOT included in a 'complaint'?
Where must an appeal against a sentence imposed by a Second Class Magistrate be filed?
If the Public Prosecutor withdraws a case with the court's permission after framing of charge, the accused shall be entitled to ________.
X contracts to sell a cow to Y but refuses to perform. Y sues. The appropriate remedy for Y is ________.
Under which section of the Specific Relief Act, 1877 is an unregistered contract for sale of immovable property not specifically enforceable?
X sells land to Y, knowing that others have a right of way over it but concealing this from Y. What can Y do with the contract?
Which of the following is NOT correct in a suit brought under section 9 of the Specific Relief Act, 1877?

options.A: No suit can be filed against the government
options.B: The plaintiff is barred from establishing title

correct_answer_text: B) The plaintiff is barred from establishing title

question_text: The concept that a contract is not specifically enforceable if it would cause 'Hardship' to the defendant is found in which section of the Specific Relief Act, 1877?
In which section of the Specific Relief Act, 1877 is 'Preventive relief' defined?
Specific performance of a contract is not enforceable where compensation for breach of contract is ________.
X is the lawful possessor of land over which residents of a neighbouring village claim a right of way. What is X's remedy?
Under section 12 of the Specific Relief Act, 1877, in how many circumstances is specific performance of a contract enforceable?
X contracts to serve as an apprentice lawyer under Y for 2 years. Specific performance of the contract is ________.
If the total area of the plaintiff's land is incorrectly recorded in the decree, under which section of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 is there a remedy?
The provision regarding 'leave for revision' is mentioned in which section of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908?
Who can file a suit in the case of a public nuisance?
An application for 'discovery by interrogatories' must be filed within _______ days of framing of issues.
The provision for taking cognizance of a suit of a civil nature is mentioned in which section of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908?
Which of the following is NOT related to 'mesne profits'?
The court may amend issues ______.

options.A: At any time before pronouncement of judgment
options.D: At any time before passing the decree

correct_answer_text: D) At any time before passing the decree

question_text: Under Order 12 Rule 6 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, when can a 'Judgment on admissions' be given?

options.A: At any stage of the suit
options.B: After completion of evidence of both parties

correct_answer_text: A) At any stage of the suit

question_text: Under section 89A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, within how many days of the appointment of a mediator must mediation be completed?
The provision for 'representative suit' is mentioned in which Rule of Order 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908?
In a civil suit, who shall receive the costs imposed in response to adjournment applications by both parties?
How many types of issues are there under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908?
If a person fails to produce a document despite direction in a court summons, under which section of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 can the civil court take punitive action?
What is the remedy against an order rejecting an application for amendment of a plaint?
If a suit is filed without following Order 7 Rule 9(1A) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, under which provision does the defendant have a remedy?
Under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, how many times can a party directly seek to set aside an ex-parte decree?
In a civil suit, what is the maximum number of adjournments a party can receive before the final hearing?
Regarding transfer of a case by the District Judge under section 24 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, ___________.

options.A: Hearing is required but not notice
options.B: Both notice and hearing are required
options.C: Notice is required but not hearing
options.D: Neither notice nor hearing is required

correct_answer_text: D) Neither notice nor hearing is required

question_text: In the case of directly setting aside an ex-parte order, what is the maximum amount of costs (in Taka) the court can order to be paid?
In which of the following circumstances can the civil court NOT exercise its inherent powers?

options.B: To prevent abuse of court process
options.D: To overcome the bar of limitation

correct_answer_text: D) To overcome the bar of limitation

question_text: The burden of proving a 'Plea of alibi' lies on —
The exception to the rule that leading questions cannot be put to one's own witness is described in which section of the Evidence Act, 1872?
The principle of 'Feeding the grant by estoppel' is related to which section of the Evidence Act, 1872?
Under the Evidence Act, 1872, which of the following is NOT a public document?

options.A: Statement recorded by a Magistrate in a departmental inquiry

correct_answer_text: A) Statement recorded by a Magistrate in a departmental inquiry

question_text: Under the Evidence Act, 1872, which of the following is NOT an electronic record?
Under which section of the Evidence Act, 1872 is a Judge not compelled to answer questions concerning matters that came to his knowledge without an order from a superior court?
'No new trial for improper admission or rejection of evidence' — this provision of the Evidence Act, 1872 applies to ________.
Who can prohibit putting of indecent or annoying questions to a witness?
Under section 145 of the Evidence Act, 1872, the purpose of cross-examining a witness about a previous written statement is to ______.
Under section 59 of the Evidence Act, 1872, all facts except ______ may be proved by oral evidence.
Under section 63 of the Evidence Act, 1872, which of the following is NOT 'secondary evidence'?

options.D: Oral account of a person who has seen the document

correct_answer_text: C) Carbon copy of original document

question_text: Under the Evidence Act, 1872, regarding the execution of a Mukharnama (power of attorney), the court shall ___________.
Under section 28 of the Evidence Act, 1872, a confession made after removal of the impression caused by promise, threat or inducement is ______.
Under the Evidence Act, 1872, an admission is _________ regarding the matter admitted.
In which section of the Evidence Act, 1872 is the competency of witnesses discussed?
How many ways of making a false document are mentioned in the Penal Code, 1860?
For which offence shall imprisonment be without hard labour?
Under the Penal Code, 1860, a mark used on goods to indicate they were produced by a specific person is called a ______.
In which section of the Penal Code, 1860 is the definition of unlawful assembly found?
If a person forges a document knowing it will be used for fraud, the maximum imprisonment is ______ years.
The maximum imprisonment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons is ______.
The maximum imprisonment for giving false evidence in a judicial proceeding is _______.
Under the Penal Code, 1860, for which offence is no specific amount of fine mentioned?
Under the Penal Code, 1860, what is the maximum term of imprisonment for criminal breach of trust by a public servant?
Under the Penal Code, 1860, theft relates to ________ property.
For falsely using another person's name to make a declaration in a civil matter, the maximum imprisonment is ________ years.
Which offence carries the highest maximum term of imprisonment?
Under the Penal Code, 1860, in how many ways can the offence of 'House-breaking' be committed?
Under the Penal Code, 1860, compelling a person by force to move from a place is called ________.
Which of the following is NOT culpable homicide?

options.B: Act done with intention of causing death
options.C: Act done knowing death is likely

correct_answer_text: D) Death of an unborn child

question_text: Under the Penal Code, 1860, sedition is _________.

options.B: Hatred or contempt towards the Government

correct_answer_text: B) Hatred or contempt towards the Government

question_text: In which section of the Penal Code, 1860 is the punishment for criminal intimidation found?
Under section 97 of the Penal Code, 1860, for which offence does a person have the right to defend property?
Which section of the Penal Code, 1860 deals with dishonestly retaining property knowing it to be stolen?
According to the Penal Code, 1860, how many types of kidnapping are there?
'A lawyer who performed any work while holding a public office cannot engage in the same matter after retirement.' — This reflects a lawyer's duty of conduct towards whom?
Which Article of the Bangladesh Legal Practitioners and Bar Council Order, 1972 is 'anti-gender discrimination'?
Who nominates the Chairman of the 'Enrolment Committee'?
If a lawyer becomes a witness on non-formal matters in a case for his client, the lawyer shall ___________ the case.

options.D: Transfer the conduct to another lawyer

correct_answer_text: D) Transfer the conduct to another lawyer

question_text: What is the primary duty of a Public Prosecutor?

options.A: Suppressing facts favourable to the accused
options.B: Always ensuring victory for the state

question_text: The provision regarding 'legal disability' in the Limitation Act, 1908 does not apply to which type of suit?
The limitation for a suit for cancellation of a document is described in which Article of the First Schedule of the Limitation Act, 1908?
Which section of the Limitation Act, 1908 contains the provision for computing the time mentioned in an instrument?
X, aged 14, acquires the right to sue for enforcement of a contract in 2014. X's limitation period will expire in the year __________.
What is the limitation period for filing a suit for declaration of title?
What is the limitation period for a suit for damages due to malicious prosecution?
What is the limitation period for filing an application to restore an appeal dismissed for non-prosecution?
What is the limitation period for a suit to set aside a fraudulent decree?
What is the limitation period for a suit to recover a lawyer's fees in any case?
What is the limitation period for a suit for relief on the ground of mistake?
